text.title: Trivy Pinned Supply-Chain Scan Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed when deploying Trivy + SBOM. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Workflow references `aquasecurity/trivy-action@v0.30.0`; tag was retroactively repointed to malicious commit; CI ran malicious code.
antipattern.root-cause: Tag-based pinning is mutable; SHA-based is immutable.
antipattern.fix: Replace every tag reference with full 40-char SHA; cross-check SHA against compromised registry; commit `compromised-tags.blocklist` so CI fails any future reintroduction.

antipattern.symptom: Trivy gate set to fail on MEDIUM+; CI fails 5x/day on noise; team disables the check.
antipattern.root-cause: MEDIUM has too much noise for a blocking gate.
antipattern.fix: Set blocking gate to HIGH,CRITICAL only; route MEDIUM/LOW to advisory PR comment; revisit threshold quarterly.

antipattern.symptom: Release tagged; downstream EU CRA compliance audit asks for SBOM; nobody generated one.
antipattern.root-cause: Release pipeline lacked SBOM emission step.
antipattern.fix: Add `release-sbom.sh` step running `trivy fs --format cyclonedx --output sbom.cdx.json .` + `gh release upload --clobber $TAG sbom.cdx.json`; require the step in release workflow.
